title: The Scale Journey
concept: Tracing the scale progression: GPT-1 (117M params) -> GPT-2 (1.5B) -> GPT-3 (175B) -> modern frontier models scaling to trillions of parameters.

exercises.prompt: What happens to an AI model as we increase its number of parameters?

options.text: The model generally becomes smarter and gains new skills.
The model becomes smaller and forgets how to read.

exercises.explain: Parameters act as the memory and settings of an AI. Generally, the more parameters a model has, the smarter and more capable it becomes at complex tasks.

cards.title: The Super-Sizing of AI
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI went from writing weird, broken sentences to drafting full essays and writing computer code in just a few years? The secret isn't a magical new formula—it's mostly size! By making AI models thousands of times larger, they unlocked mind-blowing abilities. Let's trace this epic scale journey!

cards.title: What is a Parameter?
cards.body: Parameters are the adjustable 'dials' or 'knobs' inside an AI model. When we train a model, we tune these dials so it can make smart decisions. Scaling up means adding more dials: GPT-1 had 117 million dials, GPT-2 had 1.5 billion, GPT-3 jumped to 175 billion, and today's top models use trillions of dials to understand the world.

cards.title: The Building Block Metaphor
cards.body: Imagine AI models as buildings. GPT-1 is a cozy family home (117 million bricks). GPT-2 is a grand 10-story apartment block (1.5 billion bricks). GPT-3 is a massive, towering skyscraper (175 billion bricks). And modern frontier models are like entire mega-cities spanning trillions of bricks! The more bricks we use to build, the more people and activities the city can support.

cards.title: The Timeline of Growth
cards.body:
Let's look at the actual numbers of the scale journey:
• GPT-1 (2018): 117 Million parameters. It could read books and predict the next word.
• GPT-2 (2019): 1.5 Billion parameters. It could write believable paragraphs.
• GPT-3 (2020): 175 Billion parameters. It could write essays, code, and translate languages.
• Modern Models (Today): Trillions of parameters. They can analyze videos, solve complex math, and act as virtual assistants.

cards.title: Myth: Size Only Affects Speed
cards.body: You might think that making a model bigger just makes it write faster. In reality, size changes everything! When models cross certain size thresholds, they suddenly show 'emerging abilities'—skills like logic, translation, and coding that they were never explicitly programmed to do. It is a change in quality, not just speed.

cards.title: The Scale Leap: GPT-2 vs GPT-3
cards.body: Compare the dramatic leap in scale between GPT-2 and GPT-3. The difference in parameter count represents a massive leap in what the AI can understand and do.

right.text: 175 Billion parameters. A 100x increase that unlocked coding, translation, and basic reasoning skills.

left.text: 1.5 Billion parameters. Good at generating simple text, but struggles with complex reasoning and logic.

exercises.prompt: Which early milestone model shook the tech world with its massive scale of 175 billion parameters?
exercises.explain: GPT-3 was a massive milestone released in 2020, boasting 175 billion parameters, which was over 100 times larger than its predecessor, GPT-2.

exercises.prompt: True or False: GPT-1 was larger and had more parameters than GPT-3.
exercises.explain: GPT-1 was actually the smallest of the three, with only 117 million parameters. GPT-3 is much larger with 175 billion parameters.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of today's largest AI models.
exercises.sentence: While GPT-1 had millions of parameters, modern frontier models have scaled to include _ of parameters.
exercises.explain: Modern frontier AI models have scaled way past millions and billions, now containing trillions of parameters to handle highly complex tasks.

exercises.prompt: Which class of AI models has the highest capacity and uses trillions of parameters?
exercises.explain: Modern frontier models are extremely large, utilizing trillions of parameters to achieve advanced reasoning, coding, and multi-modal skills.

exercises.prompt: Look back at the journey from GPT-1 to GPT-3 and beyond. Which statement best describes this massive scaling journey?

options.text: They remained exactly the same in capability but became much faster to train.
They shrunk in size while keeping the exact same performance.
They grew over 1000x in size and unlocked completely new skills like coding and reasoning.
They were replaced by simple rules that do not require parameters.

exercises.explain: Going from GPT-1 (117M) to GPT-2 (1.5B) and then GPT-3 (175B) represents an increase of over 1000x in size. As we scaled up, these models didn't just get better at their old tasks—they unlocked entirely new capabilities, like writing code and reasoning, that they couldn't do before!
